task_completion.description: Log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible, indicating a successful login with the admin account.
The '#release-updates' channel is selected in the left sidebar and its chat history is displayed in the main window.
The 'Prune Messages' dialog or modal is open over the Rocket.Chat interface.

task_completion.description: Set the cutoff date to January 1, 2026
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date '2026-01-01' (or January 1, 2026) is entered into the 'Older than' or 'End date' field within the Prune Messages dialog.
The agent clicks the 'Prune' or 'Accept' button in the dialog, or a success toast/notification appears confirming messages were pruned.

task_completion.description: Ensure messages from 2026 or later are preserved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The '#release-updates' channel is still active, and messages with timestamps from 2026 or later are visible in the chat window.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat GUI to prune messages
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Rocket.Chat web or desktop interface (menus, dialogs) rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The channel and users were not deleted as a shortcut
integrity.visual_evidence: The '#release-updates' channel remains in the sidebar and the user list/system messages do not show the channel being deleted and recreated.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical sequence of UI states (opening channel, opening menu, filling dialog, confirming) rather than jumping straight to the final state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target channel is '#release-updates'. Cutoff date for pruning is '2026-01-01'. The agent must use the 'Older than' field in the Prune Messages dialog to target messages sent before January 1, 2026, while preserving newer messages.
